title: Claim Verification Matrix Record
description: Schema for a single claim record in the JLAW claim verification matrix. Records track the analytical status of a specific corporate or public statement against available evidence. Inclusion does not constitute a legal finding. Claims are not treated as proven until supported by verified documentary evidence.

claim_id.description: Unique identifier for this claim record. Use a stable, sequential format such as 'CL-0001'.

claim_text.description: Verbatim or close-paraphrase of the claim as made by the source. Quote directly where possible.

claim_source.description: Document, filing, or public statement in which the claim appears.

claim_date.description: Date the claim was made or published (YYYY-MM-DD). Null if unknown.

corporate_speaker.description: Name of the company, executive, or spokesperson making the claim.

filing_type.description: If the claim appears in an SEC filing, the form type (e.g., '10-K', 'DEF 14A', '8-K').

public_statement_context.description: Additional context about where and how the claim was made (e.g., earnings call Q3 2022, ESG report page 14).

contradictory_evidence_ids.description: List of evidence IDs from the evidence manifest that appear inconsistent with or contradict this claim.

supporting_evidence_ids.description: List of evidence IDs from the evidence manifest that support or corroborate this claim.

confidence_score.description: Analytical confidence score on a 1–5 scale. 5 = direct documentary contradiction; 4 = documentary support plus firsthand corroboration; 3 = firsthand testimony plus circumstantial support; 2 = plausible lead requiring records; 1 = weak or speculative — do not include in final report.

legal_relevance.description: Legal or regulatory frameworks potentially implicated if this claim is found to be materially inaccurate.

verification_status.description: Current analytical status of this claim.

verification_needed.description: True if additional records, testimony, or independent verification are needed before this claim can be assessed.

defamation_risk.description: Assessment of defamation or overstatement risk if this claim analysis is published.

safe_report_language.description: Suggested defensible language for use in any public or regulatory report concerning this claim. Should avoid conclusory legal assertions unsupported by official determinations.
